Spectra Modules¶

Abstract interfaces for spectra and phase-space-factor computations.

class spades.spectra.base.SpectrumBase(total_ke, ei_ef)[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Base interface shared by all spectra implementations.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute a 1D spectrum for the requested spectrum type.

Args:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes): Spectrum type selector.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

abstract compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute a 2D spectrum for the requested spectrum type.

Args:

sp_type (ph.SpectrumTypes): Spectrum type selector.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

abstract compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute phase-space factors from integrated spectra.

abstract integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate previously computed spectrum values.

class spades.spectra.base.BetaSpectrumBase(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions)[source]¶

Bases: SpectrumBase

Base class for channels with outgoing beta particles.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute a 1D beta spectrum for sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

abstract compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute a 2D beta spectrum for sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

abstract compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute PSFs from integrated spectra.

abstract integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate and normalize previously computed spectra.

Numba-accelerated closure kernels for neutrino-energy integrands.

spades.spectra.closure_helpers.kn(e1, e2, enu1, enu2, enei)¶

Return the K closure denominator combination.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • enu1 (float) –

  • enu2 (float) –

  • enei (float) –

spades.spectra.closure_helpers.ln(e1, e2, enu1, enu2, enei)¶

Return the L closure denominator combination.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • enu1 (float) –

  • enu2 (float) –

  • enei (float) –

spades.spectra.closure_helpers.neutrino_integrand_closure_standard_00(enu1, e1, e2, enu2, enei)¶

Compute standard closure neutrino integrand for 0+ -> 0+ like channels.

Parameters:

  • enu1 (float) –

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • enu2 (float) –

  • enei (float) –

spades.spectra.closure_helpers.neutrino_integrand_closure_standard_02(enu1, e1, e2, enu2, enei)¶

Computes the neutrino integrant for standard (i.e. “G”) psfs/spectra in closure approximation for the 0+ -> 2+ transition.

Args:

enu1 (float): energy of first (anti-)neutrino: over this we integrate e1 (float): total energy of first electron (positron) e2 (float): total energy of second electron (positron) enu2 (float): energy of second (anti-)neutrino. enei (float): <E_N> - E_I

Returns:

float: value of integrant

Parameters:

  • enu1 (float) –

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • enu2 (float) –

  • enei (float) –

Return type:

float

spades.spectra.closure_helpers.neutrino_integrand_closure_angular_00(enu1, e1, e2, enu2, enei)¶

Computes the neutrino integrant for “Angular” (i.e. H) psfs/spectra in closure approximation for the 0+ -> 2+ transition.

Args:

enu1 (float): energy of first (anti-)neutrino: over this we integrate e1 (float): total energy of first electron (positron) e2 (float): total energy of second electron (positron) enu2 (float): energy of second (anti-)neutrino. enei (float): <E_N> - E_I

Returns:

float: value of integrant

Parameters:

  • enu1 (float) –

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • enu2 (float) –

  • enei (float) –

Return type:

float

spades.spectra.closure_helpers.neutrino_integrand_closure_angular_02(enu1, e1, e2, enu2, enei)¶

Computes the neutrino integrant for “Angular” (i.e. H) psfs/spectra in closure approximation for the 0+ -> 2+ transition.

Args:

enu1 (float): energy of first (anti-)neutrino: over this we integrate e1 (float): total energy of first electron (positron) e2 (float): total energy of second electron (positron) enu2 (float): energy of second (anti-)neutrino. enei (float): <E_N> - E_I

Returns:

float: value of integrant

Parameters:

  • enu1 (float) –

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • enu2 (float) –

  • enei (float) –

Return type:

float

Taylor-method analytic neutrino integrals for spectra and PSFs.

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.small_a(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Return a = Q - e1 - e2 notation used in the Taylor expansion.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.small_b(e1, e2)¶

Return b = e1 - e2 notation used in the Taylor expansion.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order_0_00(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Analytic order-0 integral for 0+ -> 0+ transitions.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order_2_00(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Analytic order-2 integral for 0+ -> 0+ transitions.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order_22_00(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Analytic order-22 integral for 0+ -> 0+ transitions.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order_4_00(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Analytic order-4 integral for 0+ -> 0+ transitions.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order_22_02(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Analytic order-22 integral for 0+ -> 2+ transitions.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order_6_02(e1, e2, total_ke)¶

Analytic order-6 integral for 0+ -> 2+ transitions.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

spades.spectra.taylor_helpers.integral_order(e1, e2, total_ke, order, transition_type)¶

Dispatch to the proper analytic Taylor integral by order and transition.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) –

  • e2 (float) –

  • total_ke (float) –

  • order (TaylorOrders) –

  • transition_type (TransitionTypes) –

Spectra and PSF models for two-beta decay channels.

spades.spectra.twobeta.standard_electron_integrant_2nubb(e1, e2, fermi_func)[source]¶

Evaluate the electron/positron kinematic integrand for G-type observables.

Parameters:

  • e1 – Kinetic energy of the first lepton (MeV in internal units).

  • e2 – Kinetic energy of the second lepton (MeV in internal units).

  • fermi_func (Callable) – Callable that returns the Fermi-function factor at a given kinetic energy.

Returns:

Value of the phase-space kinematic prefactor including Fermi functions.

Return type:

float

spades.spectra.twobeta.spectrum_integrant_closure_2nubb(enu, e2, e1, total_ke, sp_type, emin, enei, full_func, transition)[source]¶

Closure-approximation integrand for 2nu two-beta spectra.

Parameters:

  • enu (float) – Energy of the first neutrino/antineutrino.

  • e2 (float) – Kinetic energy of the second charged lepton.

  • e1 (float) – Kinetic energy of the first charged lepton (or summed variable for SUMMEDSPECTRUM path).

  • total_ke (float) – Total kinetic energy available to leptons.

  • sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Target spectrum type.

  • emin (float) – Minimum charged-lepton kinetic energy used in integrations.

  • enei (float) – Closure parameter <E_N> - E_I.

  • full_func (Callable) – Callable including Fermi and optional correction factors.

  • transition (TransitionTypes) – Nuclear transition type.

Returns:

Value of the full differential integrand.

Return type:

float

spades.spectra.twobeta.range_enu(e2, e1, total_ke, sp_type, emin, enei, full_func, transition)[source]¶

Return integration bounds for neutrino energy in closure integrations.

Parameters:

  • e2 (float) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • e1 (float) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • total_ke (float) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • emin (float) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • enei (float) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • full_func (Callable) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

  • transition (TransitionTypes) – Arguments required by scipy.integrate.nquad() range callbacks.

Returns:

Two-element list [lower, upper] with neutrino-energy bounds.

Return type:

list[float]

spades.spectra.twobeta.range_e2(e1, total_ke, sp_type, emin, enei, full_func, transition)[source]¶

Return integration bounds for the second charged lepton kinetic energy.

Parameters:

  • e1 (float) – Kinetic energy of the first charged lepton.

  • total_ke (float) – Total available kinetic energy.

  • sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Spectrum type.

  • emin (float) – Lower kinetic-energy cutoff used by SPADES integrations.

  • enei (float) – Unused placeholders required by scipy.integrate.nquad().

  • full_func (Callable) – Unused placeholders required by scipy.integrate.nquad().

  • transition (TransitionTypes) – Unused placeholders required by scipy.integrate.nquad().

Returns:

Two-element list [lower, upper] for the second-energy integral.

Return type:

list[float]

class spades.spectra.twobeta.TwoBetaSpectrumBase(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BetaSpectrumBase

Base class for the 2betaPlus and 2betaMinus spectra computation.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 1D spectrum for spectrum type sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

abstract compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 2D spectrum for spectrum type sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

abstract compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute PSFs from integrated spectra.

abstract integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate and normalize computed spectra.

class spades.spectra.twobeta.TaylorSpectrumBase(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, taylor_order, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: TwoBetaSpectrumBase

Base implementation for Taylor-expansion 2nu spectra.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • taylor_order (TaylorOrders) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 1D Taylor spectrum for spectrum type sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

abstract compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 2D Taylor spectrum for spectrum type sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate and normalize each computed 1D spectrum.

compute_psf()[source]¶

Convert integrated spectra to PSFs in user time units.

spades.spectra.twobeta.spectrum_integrant_taylor_2nubb(e2, e1, total_ke, sp_type, transition_type, order, full_func, emin)[source]¶

Taylor-expansion integrand used for 2nu two-beta spectra.

Parameters:

  • e2 – Lepton kinetic-energy variables used by the corresponding spectrum definition.

  • e1 – Lepton kinetic-energy variables used by the corresponding spectrum definition.

  • total_ke (float) – Total available kinetic energy.

  • sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Requested spectrum type.

  • transition_type (TransitionTypes) – Nuclear transition type.

  • order (TaylorOrders) – Taylor expansion order.

  • full_func (Callable) – Fermi/correction factor callable.

  • emin (float) – Lower kinetic-energy cutoff.

Returns:

Integrand value for the requested spectrum type.

Return type:

float

class spades.spectra.twobeta.TaylorSpectrum2nu(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, taylor_order, eta_total, transition, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: TaylorSpectrumBase

Taylor-expansion implementation for 2nu two-beta spectra.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • taylor_order (TaylorOrders) –

  • eta_total (Callable | None) –

full_func(x, sp_type)[source]¶

Return energy-dependent Fermi/correction factor for a given spectrum type.

compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute the selected 1D Taylor spectrum over the configured energy grid.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

2D spectra are not implemented for Taylor 2nu in this class.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

class spades.spectra.twobeta.ClosureSpectrumBase(total_ke, ei_ef, enei, fermi_functions, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Base Closure spectrum class

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • enei (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 1D closure spectrum for spectrum type sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

abstract compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 2D closure spectrum for spectrum type sp_type.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate and normalize each computed closure spectrum.

compute_psf()[source]¶

Convert integrated closure spectra to PSFs in user time units.

class spades.spectra.twobeta.ClosureSpectrum2nu(total_ke, ei_ef, enei, fermi_functions, eta_total, transition, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ClosureSpectrumBase

Concrete implementation for closure spectrum for 2nu 2beta (plus or minus) decays.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • enei (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • eta_total (Callable | None) –

  • transition (int) –

full_func(x, sp_type)[source]¶

Return Fermi and correction factor for one outgoing-lepton kinetic energy.

compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 1D closure spectrum by nested neutrino/lepton integration.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute one 2D closure spectrum on pre-configured (e1, e2) meshgrids.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

spades.spectra.twobeta.standard_electron_integrant_0nubb(e1, total_ke, fermi_func)[source]¶

Evaluate the two-lepton kinematic prefactor for 0nu modes.

Parameters:

  • e1 – Kinetic energy of the first lepton.

  • total_ke – Total available kinetic energy.

  • fermi_func (Callable) – Callable returning the Fermi/correction factor at a given energy.

Returns:

Integrand value after enforcing e2 = total_ke - e1.

Return type:

float

class spades.spectra.twobeta.ClosureSpectrum0nu_LNE(total_ke, ei_ef, nuclear_radius, fermi_functions, eta_total, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ClosureSpectrumBase

Closure model for neutrinoless 2-beta with light-neutrino exchange.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • eta_total (Callable | None) –

full_func(x, sp_type, tr_type=TransitionTypes.ZEROPLUS_TO_ZEROPLUS)[source]¶

Return Fermi and correction factor for a given kinetic energy and spectrum type.

Parameters:

tr_type (TransitionTypes) –

compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute 0nu closure spectrum over the configured 1D energy grid.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type, e1_grid, e2_grid)[source]¶

Not implemented for this 0nu closure class.

Parameters:

  • sp_type (int) –

  • e1_grid (ndarray) –

  • e2_grid (ndarray) –

Spectra and PSFs for electron-capture plus beta-plus channels.

spades.spectra.ecbeta.neutrino_integrand_closure(enu_1, e_positron, e_electron, enu_2, enei, transition)[source]¶

Dispatch closure neutrino kernel according to transition type.

Parameters:

  • enu_1 – Energies entering the closure neutrino kernel.

  • e_positron – Energies entering the closure neutrino kernel.

  • e_electron – Energies entering the closure neutrino kernel.

  • enu_2 – Energies entering the closure neutrino kernel.

  • enei – Energies entering the closure neutrino kernel.

  • transition (TransitionTypes) – Nuclear transition type.

Returns:

Kernel value for the chosen transition.

Return type:

float

class spades.spectra.ecbeta.ECBetaSpectrumBase(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BetaSpectrumBase

Base class for the computation of 2nu EC betaPlus spectra.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type=SpectrumTypes.SINGLESPECTRUM)[source]¶

Compute EC beta-plus spectrum values.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

abstract integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate and normalize EC beta-plus spectra.

abstract compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute EC beta-plus PSFs from integrals.

compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

2D EC beta-plus spectra are not implemented.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

class spades.spectra.ecbeta.ClosureSpectrum(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, enei, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ECBetaSpectrumBase

Base Closure spectrum for 2nu ec betaPlus spectra.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • enei (float) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute closure EC beta-plus spectrum values.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Integrate and normalize each shell-resolved positron spectrum.

compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute one PSF per capture shell from integrated spectra.

class spades.spectra.ecbeta.ClosureSpectrum2nu(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, enei, transition_type, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ClosureSpectrum

Concrete class for the computation of closure spectrum in 2nu EC betaPlus decay.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • enei (float) –

  • transition_type (TransitionTypes) –

compute_spectrum(sp_type=SpectrumTypes.SINGLESPECTRUM)[source]¶

Compute shell-resolved 2nu EC beta-plus positron spectra.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Kept for interface compatibility. The implementation computes the physical single-spectrum channel.

class spades.spectra.ecbeta.ECBetaSpectrumBase0nu(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: BetaSpectrumBase

Base class for the computation of 2nu EC betaPlus spectra.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type=SpectrumTypes.SINGLESPECTRUM)[source]¶

Compute 0nu EC beta-plus shell integrals.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

0nu EC beta-plus classes store direct shell integrals and do not integrate spectra.

abstract compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute 0nu EC beta-plus PSFs.

compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

2D EC beta-plus spectra are not implemented.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

class spades.spectra.ecbeta.ClosureSpectrum0nu(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, enei, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ECBetaSpectrumBase0nu

Base Closure spectrum for 0nu ec betaPlus spectra.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • enei (float) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute closure 0nu EC beta-plus spectrum values.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

compute_psf()[source]¶

Compute one PSF per shell from pre-computed 0nu integrals.

class spades.spectra.ecbeta.ClosureSpectrum0nu_LNE(total_ke, ei_ef, fermi_functions, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, enei, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ClosureSpectrum0nu

Concrete class for the computation of closure spectrum in 2nu EC betaPlus decay.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • fermi_functions (FermiFunctions) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • enei (float) –

compute_spectrum(sp_type=SpectrumTypes.SINGLESPECTRUM)[source]¶

Compute 0nu shell integrals for light-neutrino exchange.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Kept for interface compatibility. The implementation uses shell-integrated values.

Spectra/PSF building blocks for double-electron-capture channels.

spades.spectra.twoec.neutrino_integrand_closure(enu_1, e_electron_1, e_electron_2, enu_2, enei, transition)[source]¶

Dispatch the closure neutrino kernel for 2EC by transition type.

Parameters:

  • enu_1 – Energy terms entering the neutrino kernel.

  • e_electron_1 – Energy terms entering the neutrino kernel.

  • e_electron_2 – Energy terms entering the neutrino kernel.

  • enu_2 – Energy terms entering the neutrino kernel.

  • enei – Energy terms entering the neutrino kernel.

  • transition (TransitionTypes) – Nuclear transition selector.

Returns:

Kernel value for the requested transition.

Return type:

float

class spades.spectra.twoec.TwoECSpectrum(total_ke, ei_ef, bound_handler, nuclear_radius)[source]¶

Bases: SpectrumBase

Base class for shell-resolved 2EC spectrum-integral models.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

abstract compute_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

Compute shell-pair spectrum integrals for 2EC.

Parameters:

sp_type (int) –

integrate_spectrum()[source]¶

Not used for 2EC classes because shell integrals are computed directly.

compute_psf()[source]¶

Convert shell-pair integrals into shell-pair PSFs.

compute_2D_spectrum(sp_type)[source]¶

2D spectra are not implemented for 2EC classes.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) –

class spades.spectra.twoec.TwoECSpectrumClosure(total_ke, ei_ef, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, enei, transition_type)[source]¶

Bases: TwoECSpectrum

Closure-approximation implementation for 2nu double electron capture.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • enei (float) –

  • transition_type (TransitionTypes) –

compute_spectrum(sp_type=SpectrumTypes.SINGLESPECTRUM)[source]¶

Compute shell-pair spectrum integrals by neutrino-energy integration.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Kept for interface compatibility.

class spades.spectra.twoec.TwoECSpectrumTaylor(total_ke, ei_ef, bound_handler, nuclear_radius, order, transition_type)[source]¶

Bases: TwoECSpectrum

Taylor-expansion implementation for double-electron-capture integrals.

Parameters:

  • total_ke (float) –

  • ei_ef (float) –

  • bound_handler (BoundHandler) –

  • nuclear_radius (float) –

  • order (TaylorOrders) –

  • transition_type (TransitionTypes) –

compute_spectrum(sp_type=SpectrumTypes.SINGLESPECTRUM)[source]¶

Compute shell-pair integrals from analytic Taylor kernels.

Parameters:

sp_type (SpectrumTypes) – Kept for interface compatibility.

Helpers for serializing computed spectra and PSFs.

class spades.spectra.spectrum_writer.NumpyArrayEncoder(*, skipkeys=False, ensure_ascii=True, check_circular=True, allow_nan=True, sort_keys=False, indent=None, separators=None, default=None)[source]¶

Bases: JSONEncoder

Encoder for numpy arrays.

default(obj)[source]¶

Convert NumPy arrays to JSON-serializable lists.

class spades.spectra.spectrum_writer.SpectrumWriter(format='json', write_spectra=True, write_psfs=True)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class handling writing of spectra to files.

Parameters:

format (str) –

add_spectrum(spectrum, name)[source]¶

Add spectrum object to output structure

Args:

spectrum (SpectrumBase): spectrum object to be added name (str): this will become the “key” in the output

Raises:

ValueError: if the given key already exists in the output structure

Parameters:

  • spectrum (SpectrumBase) –

  • name (str) –

write(file_name)[source]¶

Master method delegating writing based on format

Args:

file_name (str): name of the output file

Parameters:

file_name (str) –

write_json(file_name)[source]¶
